details: The ‘Samudayik Sahbhagita’ scheme, under the Samagra Shiksha Programme, is implemented by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote public participation in education by forming SMC/SMDC in all government schools to actively engage the community in educational activities.
benefits: SMC/SMDC are formed in all government schools to actively involve the community in education.
The scheme provides awards and certificates of appreciation to community members who demonstrate excellence in the field of education.
The Sapno Ki Udaan program promotes innovative and outstanding work in education to the public.
eligibility: The members of the local community shall be eligible under this scheme.
SMC/SMDC are formed in all government schools to actively involve the community in education.
The regular training programs for SMC/SMDC members are conducted by the DIET.
Under Samudayik Sahbhagita, outstanding and innovative work in the field of education is promoted through documentary films, banners, flexes, and radio broadcasts.

offline: At the commencement of the academic session, SMC/SMDC is constituted at the school level, and during the entire session, quarterly meetings of SMC/SMDC members are organized.

faqs.question: What is the main objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to actively engage the community in educational activities through the formation of SMCs and SMDCs in all government schools.

faqs.question: When are SMC/SMDCs formed?
faqs.answer: SMC/SMDCs are formed at the school level at the beginning of each academic session.

faqs.question: How often do SMC/SMDCs meet?
faqs.answer: Quarterly meetings of SMC/SMDC members are held throughout the academic session to review school activities and community engagement.

faqs.question: Are there training programs for SMC/SMDC members?
faqs.answer: Yes, regular training programs for SMC/SMDC members are conducted by the DIET.

faqs.question: How does the scheme recognize outstanding contributions?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides awards and certificates of appreciation to community members who excel in the field of education.

faqs.question: How are innovative works in education promoted?
faqs.answer: Outstanding work is promoted using documentary films, banners, flexes, and radio broadcasts.

faqs.question: Is the scheme applicable to all government schools in Uttarakhand?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme is implemented in all government schools across Uttarakhand.
